info.title: Flowsy CLI for Database Management
info.description:
Provides functionalities to perform database administration and maintenance tasks for SQL
      databases through a command-line interface.

commands.name: config
commands.description: Executes tasks related to configuration options.

commands.name: example
commands.description: Shows an example of a JSON configuration file.

commands.name: providers
commands.description: Shows the list of supported database providers.

commands.name: migrate
commands.description: Perform database migrations using versioned and repeatable scripts (see Evolve or Flyway documentation).

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Configuration file. Run the command "config example" to see an example.

options.name: --config-section
options.description: Optional. The name of the section within the configuration file where the migration options should be looked for.

options.name: --source
options.description: Path to the directory containing the SQL migration scripts.

options.name: --history-table
options.description: Table that will store the history of executed migrations.

options.name: --history-schema
options.description: Schema containing the table that will store the history of executed migrations.

options.name: --before
options.description: SQL statement(s) or script file(s) (using @ prefix) to execute before migrations. Can be specified multiple times.

options.name: --after
options.description: SQL statement(s) or script file(s) (using @ prefix) to execute after migrations. Can be specified multiple times.

options.name: --out-of-order
options.description: Specifies whether migrations can be applied out of order (true|false).

options.name: --base-path
options.description: Base directory for resolving relative paths. Defaults to the directory of the first configuration file, or the current working directory if no config file is specified.

commands.name: example
commands.description: Shows an example of the expected migrations folder structure.

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Shows the logs directory location or displays formatted log entries.

options.name: --level
options.description: Log level to display (debug, info, warning, error, fatal). Shows the most recent file for that level.

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of most recent log entries to display. Default is 20.

options.name: --search
options.description: Filter log entries containing this text.

options.name: --from
options.description: Show logs from this date/time. Supports relative formats: 1h (1 hour ago), 30m (30 minutes ago), 2d (2 days ago), or absolute: "2026-01-28 10:00".

options.name: --to
options.description: Show logs until this date/time. Same format as --from.

options.name: --list-files
options.description: List all log files for the specified level [default: False]
